interactions.summary: Jiang completes the Saudi Arabia answer by warning that oil depletion or Middle East war would expose its fragility, then qualifies game theory as a guide for asking questions and doing research rather than an answer machine.
A student asks whether the fertility/GDP data is from 2009 and whether the present data has changed.
Jiang answers that the data shown is from 2024, acknowledges fluctuations, and repeats that Israel is the only wealthy nation with above-replacement fertility.

speaker_notes.note: SPEAKER_02 is a student asking a substantive data-source question.
SPEAKER_00 is Jiang answering student questions and closing class.

claims.claim: Saudi Arabia remains an outlier but is vulnerable if oil runs out or Middle East war disrupts it.
Game theory does not give final answers; it gives a guide for asking questions and doing research.
Jiang says the fertility/GDP data shown in the lecture is from 2024, not 2009.
Jiang reiterates that Israel is the only wealthy nation in the world with an above-replacement fertility rate.
claims.temporal_scope: Claim tied to 2024 data, stated on 2026-01-06.

signature_moments.moment: Jiang explicitly limits his own tool: game theory is not the answer, it is the guide that tells you what to ask next.
signature_moments.source_phrase: game theory doesn't give us the answers
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It keeps the lecture from hardening into deterministic prophecy; the method remains investigative.

signature_moments.moment: A student tests the age of the data, and Jiang pins the graph to 2024 before restating the Israel fertility claim.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: The exchange preserves chronology and gives the later episode a concrete date for the data claim.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: A fertility rate above the replacement threshold, shown in red on Jiang's map.
A methodological use of game theory as a way to generate questions and research paths rather than deliver final answers.

chronology_notes.note: A student asks whether the map is from 2009; Jiang clarifies that the displayed data is 2024. This is important for later latest-position comparisons.

uncertainty_notes.note: The Saudi Arabia risk claim is conditional and exploratory, consistent with Jiang's immediate caveat that game theory guides research rather than proving the answer.
